intended_use: BriefCase is a radiological computer-aided triage and notification software indicated for use in the analysis of frontal chest X-ray (CXR) images in adults or transitional adolescents aged 18 and olde
intended_use_full: BriefCase is a radiological computer-aided triage and notification software indicated for use in the analysis of frontal chest X-ray (CXR) images in adults or transitional adolescents aged 18 and older. The device is intended to assist hospital networks and appropriately trained medical specialists in workflow triage by flagging and communicating suspected positive cases of vertically malpositioned endotracheal tube (ETT) in relation to the carina.
target_condition: Vertically malpositioned endotracheal tube (ETT) in relation to the carina
ground_truth: Ground truth determined by 2 out of 3 majority voting of senior board-certified radiologists
other_metrics: Positive Likelihood Ratio (PLR): 10.874 (95% CI: 8.458-13.979); Negative Likelihood Ratio (NLR): 0.143 (95% CI: 0.099-0.208); Time-to-notification: 4.93 minutes (95% CI: 4.64-5.22; Median: 4.13 IQR: 0.76) vs. standard of care time-to-exam-open: 157.0 minutes (95% CI: 155.0-159.0; Median: 50.0 IQR: 156.0)
